mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in future growth.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate below a certain threshold indicates tightening labor markets. This typically precedes wage growth and increased consumer spending, which in turn drives corporate revenues and equity prices higher over the medium term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of instances where the 3-month average UNRATE drops below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with stable or low inflation suggests healthy consumer demand without immediate monetary tightening pressures. This creates a favorable environment for corporate profits and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the interaction between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL change does not significantly explain S&P 500 20-day returns, or if returns are negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital for businesses and consumers. This leads to higher discount rates applied to future earnings, exerting downward pressure on equity valuations and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive (average return > 0.5%) during periods where FEDFUNDS has increased by 0.25% or more in 3 months within a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth indicates overall economic expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits across various sectors. This fundamental improvement in business performance supports higher equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 40% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth signals increasing manufacturing activity and business investment. These are positive indicators for corporate earnings and overall economic health, leading to positive equity returns as investors anticipate improved future performance.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative following periods where INDPRO 3-month average growth exceeds 0.5% in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts indicates strong consumer and business confidence, leading to increased construction activity and related spending. This positively impacts the broader economy and equity markets, particularly sectors tied to housing and consumption.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST shows a 3-month average increase greater than 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and can lead to expectations of tighter monetary policy from the central bank. This increases discount rates and negatively impacts corporate earnings, thus leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3.0%.
